pi_items.source: Task description specifies JSON report saved to ~/Documents/cpsc_risk_assessment.json
Task description explicitly lists all three product categories for research
Task description: 'Find at least 2 specific recalls for EACH of the three categories (6 total recalls minimum)'. Real CPSC recalls verified for all categories.
CPSC recall database verified. Actual recall numbers found: 26-307, 26-293, 26-282, 25-126, 24-071. Pattern matches YY-XXXXX format (2-digit year, hyphen, 3+ digit sequence).
Task description explicitly states: 'Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Product Safety Research'
Task description: 'containing at least 6 bookmarks relevant to your research'
Task description specifies navigation to CPSC Recalls database (https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls) and SaferProducts.gov. Both verified as real CPSC-owned government domains.

privileged_info_summary: The CPSC Recalls database (cpsc.gov) and SaferProducts.gov are real government websites operated by the Consumer Product Safety Commission. Real product recalls exist across all three required categories: space heaters (e.g., GoveeLife 2024, Vornado 2025), portable power banks (e.g., INIU 2025, Anker 2025, Belkin 2026), and children's sleepwear (e.g., NewCosplay 2023, International Intimates 2024). CPSC recall numbers follow the verified format YY-XXXXX (two-digit year plus hyphen plus three or more digits). SaferProducts.gov is a searchable public database of consumer product incident reports launched by CPSC in 2011 under the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act. The task data derives from real public government sources, not synthetic data.
